task: I’ve got a branch process that can go off the rails if nobody keeps an eye on it, and I want a simple way to spot trouble early before it turns into a bigger cleanup. Can you help me map out what I should be checking during the day and what signs would mean I need to step in?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — the easiest way is to set up a **simple daily watchlist** with a few **early-warning signs** so you can catch issues before they snowball.

## What to check during the day

### 1) New files / work coming in
Look for:
- Missing required docs
- Files started without clear ownership
- Anything sitting unassigned too long
- Rush items with no note on why they’re urgent

### 2) In-progress files
Look for:
- Stalls at the same step for too long
- Repeated back-and-forth for the same missing item
- Corrections being made over and over
- Files moving forward with exceptions instead of clean completion

### 3) Pre-send / pre-close quality
Look for:
- ID/file mismatch
- Missing signatures or initials
- Disclosures/notices not included
- Numbers or terms not matching across docs
- Blank fields
- Weird edits or last-minute changes
- Anything still pending that shouldn’t be

### 4) Team follow-through
Look for:
- Same person getting stuck on the same type of task
- Items being handed off without context
- Work being “almost done” but not actually closed out
- No updates on items that should have been finished

### 5) End-of-day carryover
Look for:
- Open items with no next step
- Files left hanging overnight without a reason
- Anything that will create a morning scramble
- A growing list of “we’ll get it tomorrow” items

---

## Signs you need to step in

Step in when you see:
- **More than one file with the same issue** — means it’s a process problem, not a one-off
- **Anything sitting too long with no movement**
- **A file that’s about to go out with a known gap**
- **People skipping the checklist because they’re rushed**
- **Confusion over who owns the next step**
- **Small errors repeating in the same spot**
- **A backlog starting to build**

---

## Simple rule of thumb

If you see:
- **missing pieces**
- **stalled work**
- **repeated corrections**
- **unclear ownership**
- **anything heading out incomplete**

…that’s your cue to jump in early.

---

## Quick branch-style way to track it
You could keep it as three buckets:

- **Green:** moving, clean, no gaps
- **Yellow:** slowing down, missing something, needs a check
- **Red:** blocked, repeated issues, or about to go out wrong

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page branch daily check sheet** or a **quick manager huddle version** you can use with the team.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this is the right lane. But I want it tighter and more usable — not generic branch-speak.

Give me something I can actually run in real time:
- morning check
- midday check
- end-of-day check
- the 5 or 6 red flags that mean I step in myself
- what gets delegated vs what stays on my desk

Keep it short, plain, and in a checklist format. If you can, make it look like something I could use in a huddle without reading a paragraph.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely. Here’s a tighter, real-time version you can actually use.

## Daily branch watchlist

### Morning check
- [ ] What came in overnight?
- [ ] Anything missing required docs?
- [ ] Anything unassigned or sitting too long?
- [ ] Any rush items with no clear reason?
- [ ] Any file already looking messy before it starts?

### Midday check
- [ ] What is stuck at the same step?
- [ ] What has been bounced back more than once?
- [ ] Any file moving forward with a known gap?
- [ ] Any repeat mistakes showing up?
- [ ] Any owner unclear on next step?

### End-of-day check
- [ ] What is still open with no next step?
- [ ] What is being pushed to tomorrow without a reason?
- [ ] Any file leaving incomplete?
- [ ] Any cleanup likely to hit in the morning?
- [ ] Anything I need to flag before I leave?

---

## Red flags — I step in myself
- [ ] Same issue showing up in more than one file
- [ ] File is stalled and nobody can explain why
- [ ] Something is about to go out incomplete
- [ ] Checklist is being skipped because people are rushed
- [ ] Ownership is unclear
- [ ] The same error keeps repeating

---

## Delegate vs. keep on my desk

### Delegate
- Missing docs to request
- Follow-up on routine items
- Updating file status
- Chasing signatures/initials
- Fixing simple, known errors
- Clearing normal backlog

### Keep on my desk
- Anything about to go out wrong
- Repeat issues across multiple files
- Stalled work with no clear cause
- Confusion over ownership
- Exceptions that need a decision
- Anything that will create a cleanup later

---

## Simple rule
**If it’s routine, delegate it.  
If it’s repeating, stalled, or about to break, I handle it.**

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page huddle sheet** with **Ready / Not ready** wording.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah — that works. Tight enough.

## Branch check

### Morning
- New stuff in?
- Anything missing docs?
- Anything unassigned?
- Any rush file already looking off?

### Midday
- What’s stuck?
- What got bounced more than once?
- Any file moving with a gap?
- Same mistake showing up again?
- Does everybody know the next step?

### End of day
- What’s still open with no next step?
- What’s getting kicked to tomorrow for no reason?
- Anything leaving incomplete?
- Anything I’m going to have to clean up in the morning?

## Red flags — I step in
- Same issue in more than one file
- File stalled and nobody can explain it
- Something about to go out wrong
- People skipping the checklist because they’re rushed
- Ownership is fuzzy
- Repeat errors in the same spot

## Delegate
- Chasing docs
- Routine follow-up
- Status updates
- Signatures / initials
- Simple fixes
- Normal backlog

## Keep on my desk
- Anything about to go out incomplete
- Repeat problems
- Stalled files with no reason
- Ownership disputes
- Exceptions that need a call
- Anything that turns into cleanup later

Simple enough. Useable.
